title: Linkind Dimmable E27 Screw Bulb, 7.3W (Equivalent to 60W) E27 Edison Screw ES LED Light Bulbs, Warm White 2700K, 806LM, A60 Standard Light Bulbs, Energy Saving Screw Bulbs, Pack of 3
features: ➤ GREAT VALUE & SOFT WHITE: This 7.3W E27 Edison screw bulb features 806 lumens of light output, giving off 2700K soft warm white. With CRI80+, they display vivid and natural colour to create extra warm light effect.

➤ SMOOTH DIMMING: Dimmable between 5%-100%, compatible with most of the dimmers in the market (BUT NOT COMPATIBLE with 3-way dimmable touch lamps. To avoid flickering, please read our warm tips below before the purchase). 220 degree beam angle.

➤ ENERGY SAVING & LONG LIFE: The 6-pack A60 LED light bulbs can last for 25000 hours. 7.3W is equivalent to 60W, saving up to 90% energy and your electricity bill. Release the same brightness but cost less than your old bulbs.

➤ WIDE APPLICATION: Standard E27 medium screw base, easy to screw in and use. With 2700K warm white light, perfectly and widely used in the home indoor lighting, hotel, meeting room, offices, hospital, schools, factories and etc. No buzzing, no humming, and no flickering.

➤ TOP QUALITY & SAFETY: All of Linkind’s A60 LED light bulbs are certified by ERP, RoHS and CE to ensure world-class quality. A+ Energy Class Rated(now called ‘F’ rating based on new labelling scheme). Free of UV, lead and mercu
description: Product Description Previous page Next page Things to Know Before Buying a LED Dimmable Bulb Check Your Existing Dimmer The dimmer type and its minimum/maximum load range determine LED compatibility. There are two main types of dimmers: “Trailing Edge” and “Leading Edge” Trailing edge dimmers work best with LED light bulbs. Most leading-edge dimmers used for halogen bulbs are incompatible with LED dimmable bulbs. Do The Maths To determine how many LED bulbs your dimmer can handle, divide its minimum and maximum load by 10. For example, if the dimmer is rated for 100W-300W: Min Load: 100W ÷ 10 = 10W Max Load: 300W ÷ 10 = 30W So, the total LED load should be between 10W and 30W. Add up the wattages of the LED bulbs to check how many can be used. Note: The total LED load must exceed the dimmer’s minimum. Using an incorrect load can cause flickering or damage to the bulbs. Still have question? Due to the wide variety of dimmer models in the European market, compatibility issues are not uncommon. If our bulbs exhibit flickering, failure to turn on, or buzzing sounds after installation, please let us know. A compatibility list is available on the product detail page. You can refer to it for compatible models and brands.
